title: Avoid Using Classes and Methods Relying on Default System Encoding
description: This rule flags any use of classes and methods that rely on the default system encoding. Such usage could result in code functioning well in the 'home' environment, but breaking in environments employing different encodings. Such issues can be extremely difficult to diagnose and nearly impossible to reproduce when it's time to fix them.
recommendation: To fix this issue, avoid using classes and methods that rely on the default system encoding. Instead, use those that allow you to specify the encoding explicitly.
impact: Using classes and methods that rely on the default system encoding can lead to unpredictable behavior of the code in different environments. This can significantly degrade code quality and can lead to potential data misinterpretation or loss.

checks.message: Specify encoding explicitly instead of using FileReader with default encoding
